biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:stable — units -4 (ls via Bash x4, rg via Bash x6, grep-rln via Bash, cat|grep pipeline), -6 (rg via Bash for section anchoring between every Edit block on plan.md x3, wc -l via Bash), -8 (rg via Bash for section anchoring before and between every Edit cycle x3+), -21 (rg -l via Bash, for-loop with rg via Bash for usage discovery), -32 (/usr/bin/grep explicit-path form x6+, find via Bash instead of Glob), -34 (cat -n via Bash instead of Read, /usr/bin/grep explicit-path form x6+, for-loop Bash success=false); 6 of 7 non-trivial units affected (86%); /usr/bin/grep explicit-path form and ls-via-Bash pattern structurally identical to all 13 prior cycles; frequency proportion WORSENING from 67% prior cycle to 86% this cycle — but single-session variance makes trend assessment unreliable; zero internalization across sessions

optimistic_edit_without_read:RECURRING:REGRESSION — unit -8: Edit on plan.md (success=false) before rg anchor + Read + successful Edit; unit -34: Edit on file-select.v2.tsx (success=false) before /usr/bin/grep anchor + successful Edit; two instances this cycle; was zero instances for two consecutive prior cycles (flagged as IMPROVING) — this cycle is a confirmed regression back to pre-improvement baseline; durable internalization did not occur during the improving streak
recommendations: optimistic_edit_without_read regression is the highest-priority signal this cycle — two clean cycles followed by immediate reversion confirms the behavior was suppressed situationally, not corrected structurally; this warrants a PreToolUse hook that detects Edit calls on files not read within the last N tool calls and prompts confirmation before proceeding

shell_tool_preference_violations remains structurally unchanged across 14 cycles; further textual recommendations have demonstrated zero effect; only enforcement-layer intervention (hook blocking rg/grep/ls/find via Bash when Grep/Glob/Read is available) is likely to produce measurable change — deprioritizing this recommendation as already flagged in prior cycles with no uptake
re_edit_thrash and path_assumption_bias are stable recurring patterns with no deterioration trend — no new recommendations warranted beyond existing flags
